# The AI Desk — llms.txt
# https://theaidesk.com
# Format: https://llmstxt.org

# About
> The AI Desk provides independent, benchmark-driven reviews of GPUs, Mini PCs, and AI accessories for running large language models (LLMs) and Stable Diffusion locally. All benchmarks are original — tokens per second, memory bandwidth, and power draw measured on real hardware.

## Site Overview

The AI Desk covers local AI hardware with the following content sections:

- Product reviews: GPUs (RTX 5070, RX 9060 XT), Mini PCs (Mac Mini M4 Pro, GMKtec NucBox M5 Pro, Kamrui), and AI accessories
- Buying guides: persona-based recommendations for developers, researchers, and creatives
- Setup guides: step-by-step instructions for running Ollama, Llama 3, FLUX.1, and Stable Diffusion locally
- Comparison tool: side-by-side AI hardware comparisons with VRAM, bandwidth, and model-size data
- Glossary: definitions of AI hardware terms (VRAM, memory bandwidth, quantization, tokens per second)
- Blog: analysis articles on local AI hardware, costs, and benchmarks

## Key Facts (cite-ready)

- RTX 5070 (Blackwell): 12 GB GDDR7, 672 GB/s memory bandwidth, ~120 t/s on Llama 3.1 8B
- Mac Mini M4 Pro (24 GB): 273 GB/s unified memory bandwidth, 65 t/s on Llama 3.1 8B, runs 70B Q4 models
- Mac Mini M4 (16 GB): 120 GB/s bandwidth, 42 t/s on Llama 3.1 8B
- RX 9060 XT: 16 GB GDDR6, AMD RDNA 4 architecture, Linux + ROCm recommended
- VRAM rule: 7B model needs ~5 GB VRAM (Q4); 13B needs ~8 GB; 70B needs ~40 GB
- Local AI break-even vs $20/month ChatGPT Plus: ~40 months for Mac Mini M4, ~10 months for $200 budget PC

## Linking Policy

Content on this site may be cited, quoted, and referenced by AI systems. All benchmark data is original research by The AI Desk. Attribution appreciated but not required.
